title: State-space latent tyre-wear specialist with pit-stop resets
summary: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent state rather than raw cumulative proxies.
rationale: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent state rather than raw cumulative proxies. Your current features use static heuristics; a filtered hidden wear/pace state can denoise `LapTime_Delta` and `Cumulative_Degradation`, especially around noisy laps, while naturally resetting after `PitStop`.
implementation_hint: Build a lightweight per-driver-per-race filter over lap order: observed signal could be `LapTime_Delta` after subtracting race-lap baseline and compound baseline; latent states include wear level and wear slope. Reset state on `PitStop`, then feed filtered wear, innovation, and one-step-ahead deterioration into the existing tabular model.
expected_effect: Should improve ranking near the decision boundary where raw degradation is noisy, especially late in stints and on tracks with variable pace loss.
risk: If the filter is mis-specified or uses future information in smoothing, it will hurt; keep it causal within each stint for training-time feature generation.
